mcqs.question: What will be the output of printf("Hello World");?
mcqs.explanation: When no format specifiers or escape sequences are used, printf prints the literal string as-is. No newline is added automatically.

mcqs.question: Which format specifier is used for printing a character?
mcqs.explanation: %c is the format specifier for printing a single character in C. It expects a char argument.

mcqs.question: What is the output of printf("%%");?
mcqs.explanation: To print a literal percent sign, you must use %% because % is reserved for format specifiers.

mcqs.question: What will printf("%d", 10+20); output?
mcqs.explanation: printf evaluates the expression 10+20 first, then prints the result. The expression is evaluated before being passed to printf.

mcqs.question: Which header file contains printf() declaration?
mcqs.explanation: printf() is declared in <stdio.h> (Standard Input Output) header file, which must be included for using printf.

mcqs.question: What is the output of printf("%5d", 42);?
mcqs.explanation: %5d specifies a minimum field width of 5 characters. Since 42 is only 2 characters, it is right-aligned and padded with 3 spaces on the left.

mcqs.question: What does %x format specifier print?
mcqs.explanation: %x prints unsigned integer in hexadecimal (base-16) format using lowercase letters a-f.

mcqs.question: What will printf("%.2f", 3.14159); output?
mcqs.explanation: %.2f specifies precision of 2 decimal places, so it rounds 3.14159 to 3.14 for output.

mcqs.question: What is the return value of printf("university");?
mcqs.explanation: printf returns the number of characters printed. "university" has 3 characters, so it returns 3.

mcqs.question: Which escape sequence moves cursor to new line?
mcqs.explanation: \n (newline) moves the cursor to the beginning of the next line. \r returns to the beginning of the current line.

mcqs.question: What will printf("%s", "C Programming"); output?
mcqs.explanation: %s prints the entire string argument. It outputs all characters from the string until the null terminator.

mcqs.question: What does %o format specifier do?
mcqs.explanation: %o prints unsigned integer in octal (base-8) format.

mcqs.question: What is the output of printf("%05d", 42);?
mcqs.explanation: %05d means field width 5 with leading zeros. 42 becomes 00042 (padded with zeros instead of spaces).

mcqs.question: What will printf("%d %d", 10, 20); output?
mcqs.explanation: The format string contains a space between format specifiers, so output shows 10, a space, then 20.

mcqs.question: Which format specifier prints a pointer address?
mcqs.explanation: %p prints a pointer value in an implementation-defined format, typically hexadecimal memory address.

mcqs.question: What does \t escape sequence produce?
mcqs.explanation: \t produces a horizontal tab, moving the cursor to the next tab stop (typically 8 spaces).

mcqs.question: What will printf("%-5d", 42); output?
mcqs.explanation: %-5d left-aligns the value within a 5-character field. 42 is followed by 3 spaces to fill the width.

mcqs.question: What is printed by printf("%g", 0.001);?
mcqs.explanation: %g uses either %f or %e format whichever is shorter. For 0.001, it prints 0.001 (not scientific notation).

mcqs.question: What will be printed: printf("%c", 'A');?
mcqs.explanation: %c prints the character representation. 'A' has ASCII value 65, so it prints character A.

mcqs.question: What is the output of printf("%s", 100);?
mcqs.explanation: %s expects a char* (string pointer), but passing an integer 100 causes type mismatch - results in compilation warning/error or undefined behavior.
